description: Machine-readable forbidden patterns for the Forge proving slice. Gate runners must check these.

patterns.name: nested_shared_envelope_in_payload
patterns.description: A shared envelope must not be nested inside another shared envelope's payload field.
patterns.detection_hint: payload object contains keys: artifact_id, artifact_family, artifact_version, produced_by_system
patterns.why: Envelope nesting creates ambiguous lineage and validation bypass risks.

patterns.name: enum_redefinition_by_consuming_repo
patterns.description: A consuming repo must not redefine or extend admitted enum values locally.
patterns.detection_hint: Local code defines string literals that conflict with or extend enum values from forge-contract-core enums.
patterns.why: Enum drift causes semantic incompatibility and silent validation failures.

patterns.name: undeclared_additional_proving_slice_wire_fields
patterns.description: Admitted proving-slice envelope and payloads use additionalProperties:false. Undeclared fields must not be added.
patterns.why: Undeclared fields signal schema drift and may carry unreviewed semantics.

patterns.name: local_only_truth_promoted_by_convenience
patterns.description: local_only promotion_class artifacts must not enter the promotion staging queue.
patterns.detection_hint: staging queue contains rows with promotion_class = 'local_only'.
patterns.why: Promoting local-only truth to shared truth violates the authority boundary.

patterns.name: derived_read_model_as_canonical_lifecycle_truth
patterns.description: Derived read models must never replace canonical lifecycle truth. A read model is never the source of truth.
patterns.detection_hint: Lifecycle state is written from a derived view model rather than from canonical artifact records.
patterns.why: Read models are derived. Treating them as truth causes stale and misleading operator state.

patterns.name: family_emitted_by_unauthorized_repo
patterns.description: A family must only be emitted by repos listed in its admitted_producers in the artifact_family_registry.
patterns.detection_hint: produced_by_system does not match an admitted producer for the artifact_family.
patterns.why: Unauthorized producers break the authority boundary and traceability guarantees.

patterns.name: acceptance_and_duplicate_reconciliation_blurred
patterns.description: A fresh acceptance and a duplicate-reconciled outcome must never produce the same intake_outcome value.
patterns.detection_hint: intake_outcome = 'accepted' used for both new records and duplicate-reconciled sends.
patterns.why: Blurring these outcomes makes idempotency auditing impossible.

patterns.name: claimed_for_send_without_durable_lease
patterns.description: A staging queue item must not be moved to claimed_for_send state without a durable lease record.
patterns.detection_hint: claim_lease_owner is null or claim_lease_expires_at is null when queue_status = 'claimed_for_send'.
patterns.why: In-memory-only ownership creates ghost claims and permanent limbo states.

patterns.name: accepted_without_receipt
patterns.description: A shared artifact must not be marked accepted on the local side without a durable promotion_receipt artifact.
patterns.detection_hint: queue_status = 'accepted' but remote_receipt_ref is null and no promotion_receipt artifact is linked.
patterns.why: Accepting without a receipt makes shared truth non-auditable.

patterns.name: restricted_payload_exposed_in_broad_review_surface
patterns.description: Artifacts with visibility_class = 'restricted' must not be rendered in operator review queue summaries or detail surfaces.
patterns.detection_hint: Queue row or detail render includes payload data from visibility_class = 'restricted' artifacts.
patterns.why: Restricted payload exposure violates the visibility boundary.
